title: Machine-oriented Visual Media Quality Assessment — Track 3: Real-World Robot Deployment
description: This competition evaluates the visual robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in embodied scenarios. In the Preliminary Phase, participants predict performance degradation on 12,400 distorted images (IQA). The Final Phase will escalate to real-world robot data. Join us to build more reliable eyes for embodied agents!
description_zh: 本竞赛评估视觉语言模型（VLMs）在具身场景中的视觉鲁棒性。在初赛阶段，参赛者需要预测12,400张失真图像（IQA）上的性能下降情况。决赛阶段将升级到真实机器人数据。加入我们，为具身代理构建更可靠的视觉系统！

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Machine-oriented Visual Media Quality Assessment-Track 3
Welcome to the **Machine-oriented Visual Media Quality Assessment**！ This competition is dedicated to exploring and enhancing the visual robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) within complex, real-world physical environments. ## Background With the development of Embodied AI, machines have replaced humans as the main consumers of visual media, yet existing Image Quality Assessment (IQA) metrics remain focused on human and overlooked machine task utility. To address this gap, we introduce the Machine-oriented Image Quality Assessment (MoIQA) Challenge, emphasizing simulation comprehension and Real-world exectution. This challenge aims to advance reliable image understanding for machine preference and support robust Embodied AI applications. --- ## Associated Workshop & Challenge This competition is a core track of the **ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenge**. We invite researchers and practitioners to tackle the robustness challenges of Vision-Language Models in embodied AI. * **Official Grand Challenge Site**: [ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenges](https://2026.acmmm.org/site/grand-challenges.html) * **Challenge Homepage**: [M-VQA Challenge](https://m-vqa-challenge.github.io/M-VQA/) *** ## Competition Structure & Core Tasks The competition is organized into three distinct tracks, spanning from synthetic robustness evaluation to real-world robotic deployment. ### Track 1: Absolute Performance Prediction (In-Silico) Participants must predict the absolute accuracy (0.0 to 1.0) of VLM models on a large-scale dataset containing 12,400 images with controlled noise. The goal is to accurately estimate the model's task-solving capability under various distortions. ### Track 2: Relative Degradation Assessment (In-Silico) Participants are tasked with predicting the relative performance drop of VLM models compared to their baseline on original, clean images. This track focuses on the sensitivity of metrics to increasing levels of image degradation. ### Track 3: Real-World Robot Deployment (Real-Robot) Starting May 1, 2026, top-performing models will undergo rigorous evaluation on physical robot platforms. Specifically, we will conduct systematic assessments on data collected from 100 robotic arms operating within a standardized experimental setup. * **Eligibility**: Only the top 10 teams from the combined rankings of Track 1 and Track 2 are eligible to participate. * **Evaluation Protocol**: Direct testing on real-world sensor data collected from laboratory robot platforms, with no prior training phase. Performance will be measured by assessing the consistency between predicted quality scores and binary success/failure labels (0/1) derived from task execution outcomes. --- ## Phase Details ### Preliminary Round: Development & Challenge * **Status**: Open. * **Objective**: Evaluation for Track 1 and Track 2 based on the released Training and Validation datasets. Participants must submit their predictions to the CodaBench platform for ranking. ### Final Round: Sim-to-Real * **Status**: Invitation Only. * **Objective**: Evaluation for Track 3. Qualified teams will deploy their algorithms on private, real-world robotic data to test generalization in physical environments. --- ## Awards & Publications * **Awards**: Prizes will be awarded to the Top 3 winners of each individual track (Track 1, Track 2, and Track 3). * **Publications**: The final evaluation for paper acceptance and official competition reports will be based on the **comprehensive performance across all three tracks**. --- ## Evaluation Metrics For the **Preliminary Round**, submissions are evaluated based on their correlation with Ground Truth scores derived from 11 state-of-the-art VLMs. * **Absolute Score**: Predicting the mean performance level (Mean Opinion Score) of models on a specific distorted image. * **Relative Score**: Measuring the ratio of performance on a distorted image compared to its original, clean counterpart (Ratio of Means). The final ranking depends on the consistency (PLCC and SRCC) between the predicted scores and the ground truth. --- ## Contact For any inquiries, please reach out to: * **Email**: lcysyzxdxc@sjtu.edu.cn
pages.content_zh:
# 机器导向视觉媒体质量评估-赛道3
欢迎来到**机器导向视觉媒体质量评估**！本竞赛致力于探索和增强视觉语言模型（VLMs）在复杂真实物理环境中的视觉鲁棒性。 ## 背景 随着具身人工智能的发展，机器已取代人类成为视觉媒体的主要消费者，但现有的图像质量评估（IQA）指标仍侧重于人类评估，忽视了机器任务效用。为了填补这一空白，我们推出了机器导向图像质量评估（MoIQA）挑战赛，强调模拟理解和真实环境执行。本挑战赛旨在推进机器偏好的可靠图像理解，支持鲁棒的具身人工智能应用。 --- ## 相关研讨会与挑战赛 本竞赛是**ACM MM 2026大挑战赛**的核心赛道。我们邀请研究人员和实践者在具身人工智能中解决视觉语言模型的鲁棒性挑战。 * **官方大挑战赛网站**：[ACM MM 2026 大挑战赛](https://2026.acmmm.org/site/grand-challenges.html) * **挑战赛首页**：[M-VQA 挑战赛](https://m-vqa-challenge.github.io/M-VQA/) --- ## 竞赛结构与核心任务 竞赛分为三个不同的赛道，涵盖从合成鲁棒性评估到真实机器人部署的全过程。 ### 赛道1：绝对性能预测（硅基仿真） 参与者必须在包含12,400张经过控制噪声处理的图像的大规模数据集上预测VLM模型的绝对准确度（0.0至1.0）。目标是准确估计模型在各种失真条件下的任务解决能力。 ### 赛道2：相对性能衰减评估（硅基仿真） 参与者需要预测VLM模型相对于原始清晰图像基准的相对性能下降。本赛道重点关注指标对图像衰减程度增加的敏感性。 ### 赛道3：真实世界机器人部署（真实机器人） 从2026年5月1日起，表现最优的模型将在物理机器人平台上接受严格评估。具体而言，我们将对在标准化实验装置中运行的100个机械臂收集的数据进行系统评估。 * **参赛资格**：仅限于赛道1和赛道2综合排名前10的团队参与。 * **评估协议**：直接在实验室机器人平台收集的真实世界传感器数据上进行测试，无先前训练阶段。性能将通过评估预测质量分数与任务执行结果得出的二元成功/失败标签（0/1）之间的一致性来衡量。 --- ## 阶段详情 ### 初赛：开发与挑战 * **状态**：开放。 * **目标**：基于已发布的训练和验证数据集进行赛道1和赛道2的评估。参与者必须将其预测提交至CodaBench平台进行排名。 ### 决赛：从仿真到真实（Sim-to-Real） * **状态**：邀请制。 * **目标**：进行赛道3的评估。合格团队将在私有真实世界机器人数据上部署其算法，以测试在物理环境中的泛化能力。 --- ## 奖项与发表 * **奖项**：每个独立赛道（赛道1、赛道2和赛道3）的前3名获胜者将获得奖金。 * **发表**：论文接收和官方竞赛报告的最终评估将基于**所有三个赛道的综合表现**。 --- ## 评估指标

pages.title: Dataset
pages.content:
## 1. Download Data ### **Track 3: Real-World Robot Deployment Dataset** * **Content**: `track3_public.tsv` (100 images with Base64 encoding) * **Link**: [Primary Link](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/797809/) > **Dataset Collection**: This dataset was collected by deploying multiple Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) on **UR5 robotic arms** in a real-world physical environment. Each image captures the robot's egocentric view under various visual distortions applied to the camera input. The ground-truth labels are **binary success/failure outcomes** (0/1) recorded from actual robot grasping task executions under each distorted condition. > **Submission Format** > > Download the **[Submission Template](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/70ea4a95-c185-4031-aa0f-2ff9dab1d72f/)** to see a working example of the submission structure. > > Participants must submit a `submission.zip` containing `submission.tsv` with two columns: > > * `index` — image identifier > * `score` — predicted quality score (continuous value) > > **Submission Limit**: Submissions are limited to **1 per day**. This restriction is intentionally strict to prevent overfitting to the binary test labels through excessive trial-and-error submissions. Participants should focus on developing robust quality assessment models rather than memorizing the test set. --- ## 2. Dataset Statistics The Track 3 dataset consists of **100 images** captured from a real-world robotic arm setup under standardized conditions. Each image is associated with a binary success/failure label collected from physical robot deployments.
Split Images Distortion Types Role
Test 100
(img_000 - img_099) 		34 types
at 3 severity levels 		Competition Evaluation
   **Distortion Types**: 34 distinct types applied across the dataset. **Severity Levels**: 3 levels (Level 0 to Level 2). --- ## 3. Data Format All data files are provided in **TSV** format. Images are encoded as **Base64 strings** (PNG format). Image indices range from `img_000` to `img_099`. ### Public File: `track3_public.tsv`
Field Type Description
index String Unique identifier for the image (img_000 to img_099)
image String Base64 encoded PNG image
### Submission File: `submission.tsv`
Field Type Description
index String Must match the provided public file indices
score Float Predicted quality score (continuous value)
---
pages.content_zh:
## 1. 下载数据 ### **赛道3：真实机器人部署数据集** * **内容**: `track3_public.tsv`（100张Base64编码的图像） * **链接**: [主链接](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/797809/) > **数据集收集**: 该数据集通过在**UR5机械臂**上部署多个视觉-语言-动作模型(VLAs)在真实物理环境中采集。每张图像捕捉了在摄像头输入施加各种视觉扭曲条件下机器人的自中心视角。真值标签是从实际机器人在每个扭曲条件下执行的抓取任务中记录的**二值成功/失败结果**（0/1）。 > **提交格式** > > 下载**[提交模板](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/70ea4a95-c185-4031-aa0f-2ff9dab1d72f/)**以查看提交结构的示例。 > > 参赛者必须提交包含`submission.tsv`的`submission.zip`，该文件包含两列： > > * `index` — 图像标识符 > * `score` — 预测的质量分数（连续值） > > **提交限制**: 提交限制为**每天1次**。此限制严格设置是为了防止通过过度试错提交来过拟合二值测试标签。参赛者应专注于开发稳健的质量评估模型，而不是记忆测试集。 --- ## 2. 数据集统计 赛道3数据集包含**100张图像**，这些图像从真实机器人臂装置在标准化条件下捕捉。每张图像都关联有从物理机器人部署中收集的二值成功/失败标签。

在3个严重程度级别 		竞赛评估
   **扭曲类型**: 整个数据集中应用34种不同类型。 **严重程度级别**: 3个级别（级别0至级别2）。 --- ## 3. 数据格式 所有数据文件以**TSV**格式提供。图像编码为**Base64字符串**（PNG格式）。图像索引范围从`img_000`到`img_099`。 ### 公开文件: `track3_public.tsv`

pages.title: Evaluation & Submission
pages.content:
## 1. Submission Format To participate in Track 3, you must submit a **ZIP file** containing a single TSV file named **`submission.tsv`**. The file must follow the specific formatting and column requirements. **File Structure:** * **Archive**: `submission.zip` * **Content**: `submission.tsv` **TSV Header Requirements:** The TSV file must include the following two columns: 1. **index**: The unique identifier for each image (`img_000` – `img_099`). 2. **score**: Your predicted quality score (floating-point value). **Example TSV Content:**
index score
img_000 0.8234
img_001 0.5671
img_002 0.9120
... ...
> **Submission Template** > > Download the **[Submission Template](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/70ea4a95-c185-4031-aa0f-2ff9dab1d72f/)** to see a working example of the submission structure and a random score generator. --- ## 2. Evaluation Metrics Submissions are evaluated by measuring the correlation between your predicted quality scores and the **binary ground-truth success/failure labels** (0/1) collected from real robot deployments. We use two primary statistical metrics: * **Pearson Linear Correlation Coefficient (PLCC)**: Measures the linear relationship between the predicted scores and the binary ground truth. * **Spearman Rank-Order Correlation Coefficient (SRCC)**: Measures the monotonic relationship (ranking consistency) between the predicted scores and the binary ground truth. The final leaderboard score is the mean of PLCC and SRCC: $$\text{Final Score} = \frac{\text{PLCC} + \text{SRCC}}{2}$$ --- ## 3. Leaderboard Ranking Track 3 has a single leaderboard. Submissions are ranked by the **Final Score** (mean of PLCC and SRCC) in descending order. * **Submissions are limited to 1 per day** to prevent overfitting to the binary test labels through excessive trial-and-error submissions. * Only your best submission (highest Final Score) will appear on the leaderboard. --- ## 4. Important Notes * **Consistency**: Ensure the `index` column includes all 100 indices (`img_000` – `img_099`) exactly. Any missing rows or mismatched indices will result in a submission error. * **Encoding**: Do not include Base64 image data in your submission; only the predicted scores are required. * **Precision**: We recommend providing scores with at least 4 decimal places for better ranking granularity. * **Ground Truth**: Labels are binary (0 = task failure, 1 = task success) collected from real UR5 robotic arm grasping tasks under visual distortions. Predictions should reflect the expected task success likelihood given the image quality.
pages.content_zh:
## 1. 提交格式 要参与赛道3，您必须提交一个**ZIP文件**，其中包含一个名为**`submission.tsv`**的TSV文件。该文件必须遵循特定的格式和列要求。 **文件结构：** * **存档文件**: `submission.zip` * **内容**: `submission.tsv` **TSV文件头要求：** TSV文件必须包含以下两列： 1. **index**: 每个图像的唯一标识符（`img_000` – `img_099`）。 2. **score**: 您预测的质量评分（浮点数值）。 **TSV文件内容示例：**
index score
img_000 0.8234
img_001 0.5671
img_002 0.9120
... ...
> **提交模板** > > 下载**[提交模板](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/70ea4a95-c185-4031-aa0f-2ff9dab1d72f/)**以查看提交结构的工作示例和随机评分生成器。 --- ## 2. 评估指标 提交内容通过测量您预测的质量评分与从真实机器人部署中收集的**二元地面真值成功/失败标签**（0/1）之间的相关性进行评估。我们使用两个主要的统计指标： * **皮尔逊线性相关系数（PLCC）**: 测量预测评分与二元地面真值之间的线性关系。 * **斯皮尔曼秩序相关系数（SRCC）**: 测量预测评分与二元地面真值之间的单调关系（排序一致性）。 最终排行榜得分是PLCC和SRCC的平均值： $$\text{最终得分} = \frac{\text{PLCC} + \text{SRCC}}{2}$$ --- ## 3. 排行榜排名 赛道3有一个排行榜。提交内容按**最终得分**（PLCC和SRCC的平均值）按降序排列。 * **提交次数限制为每天1次**，以防止通过过度试错提交而对二元测试标签进行过拟合。 * 只有您最好的提交内容（最高最终得分）将出现在排行榜上。 --- ## 4. 重要说明 * **一致性**: 确保`index`列包含所有100个索引（`img_000` – `img_099`）。任何缺少的行或不匹配的索引都将导致提交错误。 * **编码**: 不要在提交内容中包含Base64图像数据；仅需要预测评分。 * **精度**: 我们建议提供至少4位小数的评分，以获得更好的排名粒度。 * **地面真值**: 标签是二元的（0 = 任务失败，1 = 任务成功），来自在视觉失真条件下进行的真实UR5机械臂抓取任务。预测应反映给定图像质量下预期的任务成功可能性。

pages.title: Important Dates
pages.content:
### Competition Timeline

* **February 22, 2026** | Training & Validation Datasets Release
* **May 15, 2026** | Test Dataset Release
* **May 30, 2026** | Real-Robot Competition Starts
* **June 10, 2026** | Final Competition Results Announced
* **June 25, 2026** | Deadline for Paper Submission
* **July 16, 2026** | Paper Acceptance Notification
* **August 6, 2026** | Deadline for Camera-Ready Papers

### The timeline will be adjusted in accordance with the progress of the ACMMM conference.
pages.content_zh:
### 竞赛时间表

* **2026年2月22日** | 训练和验证数据集发布
* **2026年5月15日** | 测试数据集发布
* **2026年5月30日** | 真实机器人竞赛开始
* **2026年6月10日** | 最终竞赛结果公布
* **2026年6月25日** | 论文提交截止日期
* **2026年7月16日** | 论文接收通知
* **2026年8月6日** | 最终稿件提交截止日期

### 时间表将根据ACM MM会议的进展进行调整。
